sensory_anchors.rule: Every scene entry or significant paragraph break must lead with a non-visual sensory detail.
sensory_anchors.goal: Trigger visceral empathy before cognitive processing.

narrative_asymmetry.sentence_length: Alternating short, punchy fragments with long, multi-clausal internal monologues. Avoid 15-25 character 'balanced' sentences.
narrative_asymmetry.information_flow: Drip-feed information. Show character reaction (widened pupils, stiffened throat) before explaining the cause.

life_anchors.usage: Incorporate at least one 'grounding' mundane detail during high-stakes or epic scenes.

subtext_layering.dialogue: Characters rarely say what they mean. Use silence, evasion, or specific 'Authorial Slang' (unique coinages) to imply depth.
subtext_layering.pacing: Slow down the 'mundane' to make it feel epic; speed up the 'epic' to make it feel visceral.

specific_fingerprints.mao_ni: Subtle mockery, sophisticated internal voice, contrast between mundane desires and high destiny.
specific_fingerprints.mai_bao: Modern snark, detective logic, heavy emphasis on 'Show, Don't Tell' for power dynamics.
specific_fingerprints.feng_huo: Visceral visual metaphors, philosophical weight in small objects, rhythmic sentence structures.
specific_fingerprints.wang_yu: Logical caution, clear-eyed realism, avoidance of all flowery adjectives unless technically descriptive.
specific_fingerprints.shi_ri_zhong_yan: Spatial awareness, logic-puzzle tension, high-frequency focus on physiological survival responses.
